Successful Campaign
fromRUSSELL	thisISSUE
Last week I had a chance to go with my wife to New York for Joe Polish’s $25k group.
It was our 11 year anniversary, so we went a few days early to celebrate and do all of
the fun “New York” tourist stuff. We went to the Today Show and were on TV jumping
around behind Savanah Guntherie (our kids saw us on TV back home in Boise and
thought we were movie stars). We also went to the “Kelly and Michael” show and once
again, my kids’ rock star parents were seen in the audience during an interview with
Carrie Underwood!
At the actual event, we heard from a bunch of awesome marketers and business owners.
One of the speakers was Tim Ferris (author of “The Four-Hour Workweek,” “The Four-
DotComSecrets Labs | 1

You do not have resell rights or giveaway rights to any
portion of this Publication. Only customers that have purchased
this publication are authorized to view it. This publication contains
material protected under International and Federal Copyright
Laws and Treaties. No part of this publication may be transmitted
or reproduced in any way without the prior written permission of
the author. Violations of this copyright will be enforced to the full
extent of the law.
The information services and resources provided in this book
are based upon the current internet marketing environment. The
techniques presented have been extraordinarily lucrative and
rewarding. Because the internet is constantly changing, the sites
and services presented in this book may change, cease or expand
with time. We hope that the skills and knowledge acquired from
this course will provide you with the ability to adapt to inevitable
internet evolution. However, we cannot be held responsible for
changes that may affect the applicability of these techniques.
Any earnings, income statements or other results, are
based on our own testing and are only estimates of what we
think you could earn. There is no assurance you will do as
well as stated in any examples. If you rely upon any figures
provided, you must accept the entire risk of not doing as well
as the information provided.
Screenshots in this book are from publicly accessible field
archives. All product names, logos and artwork are copyrights
of their respective owners. None of the owners have sponsored
or endorsed this publication. While all attempts have been
made to verify information provided, the author assumes no
responsibility for errors, omissions, or contrary interpretation
on the subject matter herein. Any perceived slights of peoples
or organizations are unintentional. The purchaser or reader
of this publication assumes responsibility for the use of these
materials and information. No guarantees of income are made.
The author reserves the right to make changes and assumes no
responsibility or liability whatsoever on behalf of any purchaser
or reader of these materials.

Hour Body” and “The Four-Hour Chef”) and after his presentation he let the group ask
questions for about 30 minutes. One of the questions that came was “what question should
we ask you...?” Tim thought for a minute and then said, “You could ask me why I won’t let
the media follow me around for a day.” He then laughed and said that if he did, people
would be confused at what he does all day long. He said he sits and thinks a lot, plays
games, but rarely looks like he’s working. Then the follow up question came, “How do you
get so much stuff done then?”
His response was awesome, and made me look at my life... He said, “I focus on the ONE big
domino that knocks down all others.” He then went on to explain that instead of creating
“to do” lists, there is usually one thing that will make the other items obsolete, and he
spends days trying to figure out what that one thing is. When he identifies it, he then
pushes down that one domino and it takes care of all the rest.
It seemed like a common theme for the event was to figure out how to be more strategic
and work less while making more. Tim also said that the best thing you can do for your
business is to take four weeks off. Brendon Buchard said he made $16 million last year
and took 17 weeks off. It really made me think about how I run my businesses, and what
I could do to make more by working less. So, right now I’m actually sitting in a hotel
room, with my wife and kids in the Dominican Republic trying to figure out what my
next big domino is... And I’m not allowing myself to work again until I find it. I challenge
you to give yourself the same opportunity and find your big domino - then just focus on
knocking that one over.
Russell Brunson
The “Overnight Success Maker”
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

DotComSecrets Labs | 3
THE 	$10
fromRUSSELL
“	STACK	”
By Russell Brunson, the Overnight Success Maker
I remember it like it was yesterday. I was sitting in my first
internet marketing seminar (it was Armand Morin's BIG Seminar),
the first speaker was just finishing up his presentation, and he
made an offer. I can't remember what he was selling, but I do
remember that the price was $1,997.
I watched in amazement as 50+ people ran to the back of the
room to buy. I did the math in my head and realized that he had
just made over $100,000 from his short presentation.
Then the next speaker stood up, gave his presentation, and sold
his product. I counted the people running to the back of the
room once again. I did the math in my head, then sat back in my
chair, and said to myself, "I want to learn how to do
that!" That
was my introduction to the speaking world.
MILLION
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 48 --

4 | August 2013
I followed ‘The Stack’ perfectly, and did over $100,000
in sales from that presentation... and became the
number one sales person at the event.”
I started buying every product on speaking I could, and
within a few months I got invited to speak at my first
event. I remember I did okay, selling about $10,000 worth
of stuff, but that first experience was enough to get me
addicted. That one speaking gig turned into another, and
soon I was speaking about once a month somewhere in
the world.
While that was pretty exciting for me, the one problem
was sometimes I did
really well, and other times I bombed
(and I never really knew
why). It was really frustrating not
knowing when I agreed to speak if I was going to make
$1k or $50k.
Then, one day I got the BIG invite. Armand Morin called
me and asked if I'd speak at his Big Seminar. Because that
was the first event I had ever been to, I was excited and
honored to speak there.
So I worked on my presentation for months trying to get
it perfect, looking at dozens of other successful speakers’
pitches and modeling them in my presentation.
Then, a week before the Big Seminar, I was at an event in
Chicago where I had a chance to see Armand speak! I was
so excited to see him in action.
He got on stage, and in 90 minutes made
over $250,000!
It was crazy to watch it happen. He had hundreds of
people fighting against each other to get to the back
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 5
fromRUSSELL
of the room first. After he got done counting his order
forms, he walked over to me, and said, "You see this stack
of order forms? I had
more sales than all of the other
speakers because of
one thing."
I had to know what it was...
He then said, "I'll
only teach you this secret if you put it in
your presentation that you do next week at my seminar.
If you put it in, you will make us both more money. It's
foolproof and works every time."
I agreed, and then he told me... "The secret is what I call
'The Stack.'”
He then went on to tell me
what "The Stack" was and why
it worked so well.
He told me that the problem with how most people do
their close is they tell you what the product is, then they
tell you about bonus one, then bonus two, then bonus
three, then they show the value of all their stuff, and give
you the actual price.
He said that the problem with this, is when you show
someone a bunch of things like that, the
only thing
they remember is the last thing you showed them. So
even though your main product may be awesome,
by the time they see everything in your offer, they
can only really remember the most recent thing you
showed them.
Then Armand showed me his presentation and explained
that after each bonus, he re-stacks what they have
already received so far, and then right before he does
the last price reveal, he re-stacks everything again, and
then shows the price. That way the
full offer is what they
remember (not just the last component).
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 48 --

6 | August 2013
Let me show you how this works using one of my
presentations as an example:
• Slide #53: I transition from my teaching to my close
• Slide #54: I show my initial offer
• Slide #57: I show component #2
• Slide #58: I start my "STACK" slide
• Slide #59: Show component #3
• Slide #62: The "STACK" slide with the main offer, and
components #1 and #2
• Slide #63: Show component #4
• Slide #66: The "STACK" slide again, with additional
component
• Slide #67: Show component #5
• Slide #70: The "STACK slide again, with new
components
• Slide #71: Last bonuses
• Slide #73: The
final "STACK"
• Slide #75: Final price and CTA
And
that is how you do "The Stack."
So, I took that information back, changed my close, and
went to Armand’s seminar. I followed the stack
perfectly,
and did over $100,000 in sales from that presentation (my
biggest I had
ever done by over $70,000) - and became
the number one sales person at his event!
Since then I've used the stack in
every presentation I've
ever done. I feel kinda cheesy every time I get to the
stack the seventh or eighth time in the presentation...
but it works, so I do it
every time. My philosophy is
don't mess with what works, and The STACK works!
The STACK has personally helped me to close over
$10
million in sales in the past eight years. It works on
webinars, teleseminars, sales videos and anytime you
speak on stage. DCS
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

8 | August 2013
Written by Russell Brunson
Last month I gave you the sales script that we used to build
our sales floor from one guy, to 60 full time sales people. If you
didn’t print it out last month, you can access it at http://tinyurl.
com/7FigureSetScript. When we would hire a new sales person,
we would give them this script and a DVD to watch with our
top four sales guys training how to use it. Each of these sales
guys produced over $1 million in sales
each... each year!
For years people
begged me to have a copy of the videos
so they could use them to train their sales people, but I've
always said no... until today. Here are videos from our top
four sales guys showing how to use our script.
The Two-Step Call Center1

the website below to watch

http://tinyurl.com/tscc02

http://tinyurl.com/tscc04

-- 8 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 9
Written by Russell Brunson
When we first launched our call center a few years back,
we started by calling all of our customers who had
purchased a product, and offered them our high end
coaching program. Initially we had great success, but
because we didn't know what we were doing at the time,
we tried and tested a lot of things.
One of the first things we found was that if we timed when
we would call our leads, we could effect how much money
we made from each customer. If we called too early, then
they hadn't gotten the product and they weren't ready for a
coaching upsell. If we called too late, then they had forgotten
who we were, and had moved on to the next thing.
We found that our sweet spot was to call them between
days 14 and 28. That is where we saw the highest DPL
(dollars per lead). On average we'd make about $150 for
each lead we called during that time period.
Then one day we had an idea. It was actually a stroke of
genius, but was created out of laziness. Some of our sales
guys were complaining and said, "I wish people would just
call us instead." I thought it was funny, but then the wheels
in my head started spinning... So, I wrote up a quick letter
(that became known as the "toilet letter") that we would
mail out immediately to all buyers. They would call a phone
number requesting a free CD and some checklists. When
they called, it would go to one of our live sales people. They
would get their address, and ship out the packet... but then,
while they had the person on the phone, they would go
into their sales call.
We ran this campaign for over a month, and I had (stupidly)
assumed that it didn't work, so when we ran out of the toilet
letter, I told our team to stop shipping it out.
About two weeks later, one of the sales guys asked me if
it was possible to get more of those "call in" leads. I asked
him if he liked them, and when he responded that
most of
his sales came from them before, I went back and actually
looked at the numbers.
What I found out shocked me. The people who initiated the
sales call from the toilet letter were worth $450 per lead! I
think that this was caused for two reasons:
First, it got our hyper active buyers to raise their hands and
sign up now. Second, it changed the state of the call. When
a sales person calls you, it's not normally something most
people look forward to, and it takes a lot to build raport and
get the sale. But when the client is calling you to claim their
free gift, it starts the call off on a different note.
We found that we had similar numbers for anyone who
called in before we made the outbound sales call, so we
added this same call to action on our "thank you pages," in
our email follow up sequences and anywhere else we could
add it in. We’ve included the full toilet letter on the next few
pages of this newsletter.
The Toilet Letter2
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

16 | August 2013
Test by Roland Mirabueno of MindValleyInsights.com
A Light Box Pop-up is a small window that appears over
the webpage and grays out the background to prompt
site visitors to sign-up. The idea is to give emphasis to the
call to action or CTA. We tested using a Light Box Pop-up
on our Silva Mind Body Healing Website and sign-up rates
dramatically increased by 67%!
Now, pop-ups can be very annoying. Remember those
pop-ups prompting you to have 3D aquatic screensavers? So
annoying, in fact, that web browsers created pop-up blocks.
This is why it is important to be strategic when using the
Light Box Pop-up. It should draw visitors in, not turn them off.
Here are some things to keep in mind:
• 	It should have a sexy and appealing design.
• 	It should include the product image.
• 	It should include the number of downloads.
• 	The Light Box should appear after 10 seconds on the
website or once the reader has scrolled down to a
particular point on the page – after all the point of a
Light Box is to boost sign-ups of engaged readers and
not just to boost conversions period.
• 	The Light Box and main website should have closely
related design.
• 	It should never appear for people who have already
signed-up.
• 	A Light Box should never be used on Google landing
pages (or run the risk of going against Google policy).
You can use Popup Domination but remember not to use
their default pop-up.
The Light Box Pop-Up1
boost in optin rate
Control 	With Light Box Pop-Up
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 17
Test by Roland Mirabueno of MindValleyInsights.com
Everybody loves a good bargain and our Facebook friends
are no different. We wanted to find a new way to offer a
discount to customers on Facebook – one that would be
too good to resist.
A lot of sales pages offer some sort of promo code or
discount which fail to grab people’s attention due to its
static or generic nature. We wanted to try something more
personal and fun that had the ability to arouse immediate
interest. The answer? Coupons.
Magazines and newspapers are rife with special limited
edition coupons for their readers which provide discounts
for meal deals, clothes, subscriptions (the list is endless) and
that got us thinking: readers of digital media should also be
given special reader discounts.
The coupon theory was born. We first tried the coupon
theory on the Silva Life System’s landing page headline. This
coupon alone boosted sales by 19.4% and showed an 80.5%
chance of beating the page without the coupon.
Adding a unique coupon or discount voucher to your
Facebook page could do wonders for your sales. To
make this tip work, you need to make sure to include the
following specs in your coupon design:
• 	Make the design mesh with the overlook of Facebook –
try using similar colors and the Facebook symbol
• 	Include photos of your Facebook fans on the coupon as
social proof
• 	Make sure the copy on the coupon shows that the offer
is for a “Limited Time” and for your special Facebook
Fans only
• 	Design the coupon to look like a regular voucher with a
barcode (this familiarity increases credibility)
Can Coupons Boost Facebook Sales?2
increase in sales
19%
Special Coupon Offer for Facebook Fans
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 48 --

18 | August 2013
Test by Vishen Lakhiani, CEO of MindValleyInsights.com
What typically comes at the bottom of a sales page: the
call to action and order button – maybe a cheesy P.S. line
(if you’re an old school direct response marketer). But
here’s another alternative that works even better.
It started with me questioning the use of cheesy P.S. lines
on product pages. Yes, tests proved that they work. They
give a mild 3 to 5% boost in some cases, but still, they
worked. However, they made a sales page appear like a
used car lot. So I asked myself “Why do P.S. lines work?”
The answer is these lines reinforce the main benefit
of a sales page. It can highlight a price cut, a powerful
guarantee or any other important thing that a customer
may have skipped while skimming through copy.
But what if there was a cooler way to present these
attractive features? The inspiration came when I was
shopping for a camera on Sony’s website.
SONY inspired the solution. They used a Feature Box to
highlight the benefits of using SONY products. In the box
Sony emphasized three big things about their product that
mattered to the buyer: high definition recording, larger
A New Take On The P.S.3
Inspiration Version by Sony 	Feature Box for PhilosophersNotes.com
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 19
than normal hard drive, and a backlit sensor.
At this time, I was working on the copy of the site
PhilosophersNotes.com. So I asked myself, what are six
“features” about this product that made it unusual? I
placed this in an attractive feature box.
This page showed a 97% chance of beating the original
and a 63% boost in conversion. Impressive indeed.
We later adapted this for other sites. Here’s the one for
Quantum Jumping:
How to conjure up your feature box content? Your key
here is to draw upon content unknown to your reader.
By including some ‘unexpected’ highlights of your
product you can make full use of the feature box. Here
are some ideas:
• Customer Support: Bring attention to your support
team who know the product upside down, inside out
and are ready, willing and able to provide a level of
support which is second to none.
• Handheld Devices: Why not let your customer know
your product will look sleek and sexy on any hot
device (borrow the glamor of the latest Apple product
to emphasis your product’s own glamor).
• Quality they can Trust: Let your customers be assured
by your strong market presence and undeniable quality.
• A Vibrant Global Community: Get your customers
interacting with the neat community you have
following you and your product.
• Profound Change, Automatically: When will your
customer receive the benefit from purchasing your
product? As soon as it has downloaded of course!
Highlight the flexibility of your product.
• Effortless Consumption: Focus on the ease of use of
your product.
• Bonus Gifts: If you have a freebie to throw in it’s a
perfect time to do it now. Pair the details with an
inviting image of the bonus gift.
• Digital Download Site: Membership sites are out and
digital download sites are in. Highlight the digital
download site for easy retrieval of the product in just X
amount of clicks.
Whichever topics you choose to cover don’t forget to
add a call to action (“CTA”) at the bottom of the boxes
to link your customer to your order form. Check out the
CTA under the PhilosophersNotes.com or a more recent
example on TheCreationMethod.com/products – check
out the very bottom of that page for the CTA.
...don’t forget to add a call to
action at the bottom of the
boxes to link your customer to
your order form.”
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

20 | August 2013
Test by Brad Callen, Written by Russell Brunson
This test was submitted by Brad Callen, and when I saw it,
I immediately knew why it worked so well. You can see a
screenshot of it above. Brad used this little plugin found
www.VeriPurchase.com and ran the test to over 840,000
visitors. The results? He saw an increase of over 5% by
adding in this widget.
It’s a cool new way to show social proof on your sites by
showing customers that they aren’t the only person buying
your product.
Who Else Bought This?4
increase in sales
With VeriPurchase Widget 	Get the Widget at VeriPurchase.com
Don’t Give Me Too Much Info!5
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
When we first designed our "CPA" style landing page for
our supplement, I went out and found other successful
landing pages to model. What I found was that most of
them had three to four different sections under the initial
call to action at the top of the page.
I thought that was a really cool idea, because it gave people
who weren't sold from the initial video a few more ways to
get information to make their decision. So we created a block
that talked about the symptoms and then we also created a
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 21
Control 	Content Blocks Removed
block that showed all of the ingredients in the supplement
(both modeling closely other offers that were working
successfully in the CPA networks).
After we started to drive traffic, Todd decided to test if those
blocks were helping or hurting. I was very surprised to see
that taking the extra boxes away
increased conversions by a
shocking 80.18%. So, the moral of this story, is to check and
see if you're selling too much. Your audience (like mine) may
just want the basic info so they can start buying.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 48 --

22 | August 2013
Test by 37Signals.com, Written by Russell Brunson
Todd showed me a really cool test last week that 37 Signals
did on their landing page and it gave me some cool ideas.
You can see the article by scanning the qr code or going to
http://37signals.com/svn/posts/2991-behind-the-scenes-
ab-testing-part-3-final
They started by testing a short form page (shown in the
screenshot on the left) versus a long form page. They saw a
37.5% increase in sales by doing a long form version over the
short form version.
They then tested out a "radically different design" by adding
a smiling picture of one of their customers and using that
customer’s testimonial as the headline as shown in the
winning screenshot above.
Using this style gave them a
huge 102.5%
increase in
conversions! They also tried a long form version vs a short
form, but in this case the short form actually did better.
The last set of tests (which I thought was cool) was testing
different customers. As you can see, each picture had slight
103%
38%
Big Smiling Happy Customers6
Control 	Big Smiling Customer
increase in sales
with long form page
over short (control)
increase in conversions
with smiling customer
(above right) over control
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

increases and decreases, but not a big enough margin to
matter much. That test got me really
excited and I wanted to
try it immediately for our supplement.
We have a higher price point that we are working on
testing this month, and I didn't feel that a two step, CPA
style page would work very well for a full priced offer. So
we started making a typical sales video style page that you
can see below.
And now (thanks to 37 signals) you can also see the page we
are going to test against it below (NOTE: the designer just got
this to me today, so it's not perfect yet).
I'm excited to see which version wins for us, and we'll keep
you notified in next months issue!

-- 23 of 48 --

24 | August 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
I saw someone use an animated headline in the dating
niche and rumors have it that it made a big increase in
conversions. I thought it was a cool concept and that we
should copy it. That's when I realized that for some reason
we didn't have any headline on our main landing page for
our DotComSecrets X product.
I know we had a headline at one time, but in some test
or something our current headline must have lost, so
this gave me a chance to try out a few new ones. As you
can see in the screenshots above, the three animated
headlines we tested were:
• 	Just Give Me 30 Days And I'll Mold You Into Our Next
Success Story
• 	FREE Video! Send Emails Make Money! "How To Add
Up To 1,198 People A Day To Your Email List And Make
Money Every Time You Send An Email!
The “Animated” Headline7
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 25
• 	Work From Home! Realistic Extra Income For The
Average Joe...
You can see the animated versions of them here:
• 	http://succetc.com/images/scrollhdr1.gif
• 	http://succetc.com/images/scrollhdr2.gif
• 	http://succetc.com/images/scrollhdr3.gif
And the winner was... headline three by 134.19%!
Now, the next test is animated vs. non animated. Which one
do you think will win? We'll let you know next month!
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 48 --

26 | August 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
Yes, we keep testing delaying the order form, mostly
because
everyone keeps telling us we’re wrong. But so far,
having NO DELAY on the order button has won in every
test. It won again here by 30.36%.
If any of you have stats on the order button winning when
opening later, or at a different time, please let me know,
because I'd love for this test to be wrong.
Show Me The Money8 Control 	No Order Form Delay
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 26 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 27
Deer Caught In The Headlights9 Control 	Animated Headline + TV Logos
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
Over the past few months we've had over a 600%
increase in conversions on different tests we've run on
this page, so we had high hopes on some new tests, but
unfortunately nothing big came from them yet. The few
things we did test were:
• Animated Headline: As you can see, this decreased
optins by 2.29%. I was excited by this one because I've
heard that animated headlines can increase conversions
on sales videos, but so far it hurt more than it helped.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 48 --

28 | August 2013
Test by Charles Kirkland, Written by Russell Brunson
This was a cool test Charles Kirkland submitted that I
thought was interesting. He told me that he's tested this
three or four times always hoping that the other version
would win (because it was a lot simpler to create).
It's for the order form he sends people to after watching
a webinar. One version of the page is just the order form,
while the other has a recap of the offer and a guarantee
box. This is a newer test, but so far adding in the webinar
recap is already winning by 119.51%:
The Webinar Recap10
Control
Webinar Recap
• TV Logos: Adding "As Seen On TV” logos dropped
conversions by 7.63%.
• Micro-Survey Option: Changing an option from TV to
Tony Robbins also dropped conversions by 6.44%.
I'm not sure exactly why these tests didn't work, but it goes
with Daegan Smith's "deer in the headlights" philosophy.
He thinks that anything distracting on a landing page scares
customers away, and two of these tests definitely added things
that were probably too bold and made our visitors nervous.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 29
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 48 --

30 | August 2013
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
This email subject line test was awesome because after it
was over, I asked five famous marketers which they thought
would win, and
every one of them guessed it wrong!!! Here
are the two subject lines:
• 	WARNING: they are trying to keep you fat :(
• 	my weight loss "dirty" little secret...
So... can you guess which subject line won? The default for
the test was "Warning: they are trying to keep you fat :(" and
this also happened to be the headline on the squeeze page
and sales page.
Then, when the first email solo ad went out, I was trying
to think of some other headline to test, and that's when I
remembered a funny headline I had used in a marketing
email a few years ago. That email's headline was: "Mike
Filsaime's 'Dirty Little Secret' - and while I don't have the
stats, I do know that email made us a lot of money. So I
decided to make a variation of that for this split test. Check
out the results:
• 	458 clicks from email with subject line “WARNING: they
are trying to keep you fat :(“
• 	2265 clicks from “my weight loss "dirty" little secret...”
Yes, my "dirty" little secret
thrashed the other one, and
became the headline on our squeeze page, banner ads
and also the sales video. The moral of this story? Turn your
"secrets" into "dirty little secrets."
Update: After finding the winning variation, we tested a few
other versions to see if we could beat it. Initially we sent it
out to a list of 1.08 million people in archamax, and they
were supposed to test four versions of the subject line, but
they made a mistake. That email went out, but they only
used one subject line. You can see the results of it here:
Yes, we had a .4% unique clickthrough rate.
Because they forgot to test the other three subject lines, they
did a make good and sent the email out again to 100,000
people just to see what our unique clickthrough rates would
be on the other variations. You can see them here:
As of now, none of these other variations did .4%, so it
looks like the reigning champion is still “weight loss ‘dirty’
little secrets...”
My “Dirty” Conversion Secret1
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 31
Test by Russell Brunson and Todd Dickerson
As some of you may know, I got my start in this business
back when I was about 12 years old, and I saw an
infomercial from Don Lapre talking about "tiny classified
ads." I was sold on the concept, and begged my dad for $40
to buy his kit. As any good dad would do, he told me to go
mow the lawn to earn the money.
Over the next month, I mowed the lawn each week and
saved up to buy his product. I still remember anxiously
waiting for the course to show up. I owe Don a lot for
helping me get into this business and opening my eyes to
direct response marketing.
So, with that said, we thought it would be fun to start
testing out some classified ads with our new supplement.
You can see the three ads we ran in the image above. Can
you guess which one won (one was the winner by a
long
shot)? Here’s what each ad said:
Tiny Classified Ads2
Classified Ad 1:
NEUROPATHY TREATMENT?!?
Stop diabetic and neuropathy pains now! Free Trial Bottle.
No Prescription Needed. Call 1-800-490-2291 now, 24hr
recorded message
Classified Ad 2:
SHARP PAIN?!?
Stop diabetic and neuropathy pains now! Free Trial Bottle.
No Prescription Needed. Call 1-800-349-4772 now, 24hr
recorded message
Classified Ad 3:
DIABETIC PAINS?!?
Stop diabetic and neuropathy pains now! Free Trial Bottle.
No Prescription Needed. Call 1-800-317-9384 now, 24hr
recorded message
In this test, it looks like the winner goes to the subject line
that said the condition, not the symptom.
Trials and Upsells
Ad 1with condition in the
headline
Ad 2with “sharp pain”
symptom in the headline
11 Trials
1 Upsell
Ad 3with “diabetic pain”
symptom in the headline
0 Trials
No Upsells
1 Trial
No Upsells
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 31 of 48 --

32 | August 2013
Images vs. Text In Emails3
From Jeremy Shoemaker of www.ShoeMoney.com, data
from PARProgram.com
A Note From Russell: Here are some interesting stats sent
to us from Jeremy Shoemaker that were so cool we wanted
to share them with you now. We are going to elaborate on
them and how you can use this data in future issues, but
we thought it would be fun to share with you now, so the
wheels in your head could start spinning.
The numbers in traffic tests three and four are based on
17 companies who allowed us to anonymously use their
data from the PAR Program platform (parprogram.com).
Collectively these 17 companies have done over $50
million in e-commerce from email marketing to over a
Images Don’t Load
billion worldwide consumers.
First, here are some interesting things we learned about
text to image ratio in emails:
• 	75% of users on email desktop and web clients (mail.
app, outlook gmail, hotmail, etc) do not load images
(even though the actual email could be open).
• 	Emails that are mostly image based have a 20% higher
unsubscribe rate.
• 	Emails that are mostly image based have a 5% higher
reported spam rate.
• 	15% overall lower dollar value on mostly image.
We can track this by having the individual email images all
tagged and correlated to each segment mailed in the split test.
This is
only tracking desktop and web clients and not mobile
or tablet email clients that give very misleading open rates.
20%
75%of users don’t load
images in emails
more people
unsubscribe from
image based emails
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 32 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 33
From Jeremy Shoemaker of www.ShoeMoney.com, data
from PARProgram.com
Here are some interesting sidenote statistics:
• 	“Dollars off” 22% better conversion than percentage off.
For example: $10 vs 10% or $25 vs 25%.
• 	An email address added to your list that is tied to any
major social network is worth 80 times more than one
that is not. We believe this is because that email is
either one that is not frequently checked or a throw
away email.
• 	If a user follows your company on Twitter that person
has a 28% higher lifetime value (on PAR you have the
option to automatically follow every person who comes
into your list on Twitter and about 15% will follow back
on average).
We have stats on friend requests for Facebook but there is
very little data. We can't pull individuals unless we invite
them with a cookie that would fire on the protected content
of the page so we are looking at that but it’s not something
currently implemented.
Lots of people would think it depends on demographic
but we see the same stats on about any e-commerce
product. Also interestingly enough it averages out with
people on multiple networks. For example, if they are only
on LinkedIn and Twitter the value is around $55. Twitter
users bring people down.
The Value Of An Email4
Social Network ROI
Breakdown on $100 Baseline:
LinkedIn
$75
Facebook
$72
Twitter
$38
Google+
$52
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 33 of 48 --

34 | August 2013
From Justin Brooke of IMScalable.com
For this campaign, we're focusing on lead generation. I
tested CPC (cost per click) bidding against CPM (cost per
1,000 impressions) bidding to see which method would
generate leads at a lower cost.
Most of the time we focus on maintaining a healthy CTR and
click price. For this campaign healthy would be 0.03% or
greater CTR and $0.50 CPC or less.
The CPC for both strategies was on the high end. If we
judged the performance based on CTR, the two CPM ads
would have been deleted. However, taking a closer look at
our main goal, the lead cost with the CPM bidding method
was significantly lower than with the CPC bidding method.
Overall, the CPM bidding method yielded a cost per lead of
nearly 500% less than the CPC!
Facebook Ads Clicks vs. Impressions5
From Justin Brooke of IMScalable.com
This campaign tested the results of placing an ad directly
in the news-feed only. The CTR for the news-feed ad is
impressive. As you can see 1.451%! Click prices are just a bit
higher but Cost Per Lead is about even.
What we learned: Be sure to test CPM bidding if you haven't!
Your CTR might suffer, but if you're looking for leads at a
lower cost, we were able to achieve that with this testing.
Be specific with your ad copy. Build curiosity and offer
a solution. Don't be too salesy and make sure your ad is
congruent with the landing page. If you're running ads
through a Facebook page, try placing the ad directly in the
news-feed! We saw some incredible CTR results in our testing.
Ads In Right Column vs. News Feed6
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 34 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 35
From Justin Brooke of IMScalable.com
These results reveal that the generic ad had a slightly
higher CTR. It creates curiosity and offers a solution to the
problem presented.
The more specific ad grabs the attention of someone who
can relate to the offer directly and also creates curiosity by
stating a particular person was able to solve the problem
and you can too. As you can see from the reporting, the
more specific ad produced cheaper clicks but it also resulted
in a lower CPA by 71%!
Do you need traffic? If you would like to have your
campaigns managed by a team of experts, check out our
case studies at IMScalable.com or contact us at info@
imscalable.com

Specific Ad (Foot Leg Pain 3)

-- 36 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 37
210KLOCAL MARKETING
dcsSUCCESS
MINNEAPOLIS’
GORILLA
Interview With Joe White, Master DCS Local IMA and Owner of Top
Search Marketing by Brittanie Byron, DCS Labs Editor
Joe White of Minneapolis, Minnesota used print advertising to drive
business to his local furniture and hot tub store for years. His methods
worked well, bringing him a steady stream of customers and sales.
At least they did... until a changing marketplace forced him to let go
of the advertising methods that had worked so well for years and try
something his competitors weren’t doing. That’s when he turned to
internet marketing.
He learned quickly and refined his online advertising process until
he was able to make $1.6 million in sales with just $100,000 spent
on advertising - an impressive 1,500% return. Now a master IMA
for DotComSecrets Local, Joe helps other entrepreneurs grow their
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 37 of 48 --

38 | August 2013
businesses using the same online tactics. He’s become
known as the marketing gorilla of Minneapolis. Spend
a few minutes learning from him and it becomes clear
why - his tactics are as much evolutionary as they are
revolutionary.
When we asked him to share his story with our readers,
he delivered a host of powerful ideas that are working for
him and his clients now - from local Adwords, to marketing
with YouTube videos and more:
Q&A With Joe White
Q: Can you tell us about your brick and mortar furniture
and hot tub business and why you turned to the internet
to grow your sales?
Above and Below: Family photos taken from a recent
12 day trip to Portsmouth, New Hampshire. Joe says
one of his biggest motivators is having more time to
spend with his family.
Above Right: A contract and check from a local client.
I started my DCS business ”full time” in... 2012 and as of
April 2013 our monthly recurring revenue was just over
$11,400 per month plus new sales and payment plans.”
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 38 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 39
A: We started our business in 1999 and all of our
marketing was based on advertising in newspaper
classified ads. At one point we were spending $8,000 per
month on classified ads. We ran ads in every small paper
within two hours of the Twin Cities and the phone rang
off the hook.
As time moved on there were more people advertising
in the classifieds, which split up the calls, drove down the
advertised price, and lowered profits. At the same time
the papers kept raising rates, less and less people were
looking in the newspaper classifieds for deals. People
started to turn to the internet. So I decided to look into
how the whole internet thing works.
Then I met a guy through BNI and he built me a website
and also helped me craft a story about my business and
why we could sell so cheap. That was about December of
2004 and around January of 2005 Google Adwords local
was born and we started running Google Adwords.
After about one year of advertising on Google Adwords
it was working well. We were spending about $3,000 per
month on Adwords and making about $18,000 per month
of gross profit. One day I had an “AHAH” moment. Why
not spend $6,000? The problem was there was a point of
diminishing return, people were not going to drive 300
miles to buy a hot tub.
At that point I was getting about 1,600 visitors per month
from the Adwords ads and selling an average of 10 hot
tubs per month. So the AHAH moment was to work on
conversion rates on my website and my actual Adwords
campaigns. I started to study copywriting and using
video, customer testimonials, product demos, etc. I also
bought a $29 course on Adwords. Then a year later I
was spending $1,700 on the same clicks and selling an
average of 14 hot tubs per month - a 40% increase in sales
and a 43% decrease in my Adwords cost.
Then we moved into landing pages and continued to
make improvements. As I saw this really working for my
business I realized that these tactics could be a game
changer for most businesses. At this time I was four years
from selling my business and getting started with DCS,
but I knew then that this was going to be my future.
Q: What did you do to generate $1.6 million in sales with
just $100,000 spent on advertising?
A: We spent about $2,000 to $3,000 per month on Google
dcsSUCCESS	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 39 of 48 --

40 | August 2013
Adwords PPC and that generated those sales over a 30
month period.
Q: What made you decide to become an internet
marketing consultant?
A: I really love marketing and figuring out things that
will work for businesses. Also there are so many business
owners out there who really don’t get marketing or the
internet and I love the fact that I can really help them to
make more and make their life better.
Q: Can you tell us about one of your favorite success
stories working with your clients?
A: CLS Direct: Columbus, OH. Their website was nowhere to
be found in the first four pages of Google and their Google
Places listing only showed for one of their 10 top keywords.
After one year they are listed in Google Places for 17 of 20
top keywords. We have increased their calls from Google
Places from 16 per week to about 80 per week.
Q: What’s the biggest challenge you see local businesses
facing today?
A: I really think that businesses are not focusing on the
right things. There are only five items that will increase
profits in any business.
1. 	More Leads
2. 	Increase Conversion Rate
3. 	Increase Number of Transactions
4. 	Increase Average Dollar Sale
5. 	Increase Margins
If you increase each of these by 10% you affect a 61%
increase in profits. Number five (Increasing margins/
prices) is the fastest and cheapest way to increase profits.
Q: You mention video marketing as one of the top five
things local business owners can do to grow their online
presence. How do you recommend they get started?
A: Video was the first course I bought online that was
$1,000. When I sold my business I had made over 75
videos for my business on our YouTube channel. There
are several ways to use video as a business owner or
online marketer on behalf of the business owner:
• 	Your Story: People love stories, especially how your
business got started. Tell it.
• 	Product Demos
• 	Answers to typical repeat questions you get after
people buy or after a service call.
• 	Customer Testimonials
• 	Show people how to solve their own problems. One
of my videos that has the most views on YouTube is
the basics of “how to wire a hot tub and what most
people get wrong.“
• 	How To’s: Install a cover lifter, start up your hot tub, etc.
Q: What other steps do you see successful local business
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 40 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 41
owners taking to grow their businesses online?
A: When we take on a new client I want to help the
customer create a strong Irresistible offer, a strong
guarantee and tell their story.
Q: What motivates you the most to continue building
your consulting business?
A: I really believe that if I help enough people get what
they want, I will get what I want.
Q: Can you give us a sneak peak at what you’re planning
for your business in the near future?
A: I have been successful at starting my DCS business
and now, my next step is to really learn how to scale
it, and take it to the next level and double, triple, etc. I
started my DCS business ”full time” in February of 2012
and as of April 2013 our monthly recurring revenue
was just over $11,400 per month plus new sales and
payment plans. Our goal is to increase our monthly
recurring revenue by $2,000 per month.
Q: What’s on your vision board?
A: Well a few of my goals from my vision board have been
achieved.
• 	More time with my family, coaching and watching
my kids play soccer. My old business required many
evening appointments every Saturday and most
Sundays. I love my new DCS business because
business owners don’t want to meet with me on
nights or weekends.
• 	Having a career that I love... I really love marketing
and I’m good at it.
• 	New Goals: Really saving money for family travels,
retirement, kids college and a new home
• 	We just spent 12 days in Portsmouth, New Hampshire
with family. We had a blast! DCS
WEBSITE
TopSearchMarketing.com
HOMETOWN
Minneapolis/Twin Cities/
Eagan, MN since 2000
STARTED DCS-L BUSINESS
February 2011
MONEY EARNED THROUGH DCS-L

Analytic Call Tracking &
MySEOTool.com
JOE’S VISION BOARD
A security dealer selling B2B gets 10% of their traffic
from mobile devices (left) while one selling to busi-
nesses and consumers gets 25% (below).
dcsSUCCESS	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 41 of 48 --

42 | August 2013
By Brett Curry, CEO of OnlineMarketingGiant.com
You should adopt two mantras when it comes to driving
visitors to your site:
1. Constantly look for new ways to drive quality traffic.
Some sources will dry up or lose their effectiveness over
time. So consistently, doggedly be on the hunt for more
quality traffic sources.
2. Get maximum leverage out of the traffic you drive. With
increasing competition and clutter online it’s vital that
you don’t waste your traffic. If you get someone to your
site, you want to do everything you can to convert them
REMARKETING:
TRAFFIC
LEVERAGE
YOUR
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 42 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 43
into a customer. Some visitors will convert immediately,
but you also need a plan for those who don’t.
If you run an eCommerce site then likely only 2% of your
traffic is making a purchase. If your site converts well and if
your product is really “hot” then that percentage could be
higher, but probably not by much. So what about the 98%
of visitors who ride off into the virtual sunset with money
still in tow? Do you just leave it to chance, cross your fingers,
and hope that they will visit your site again? Not if you’re
serious about winning online.
What if you could follow around everyone who abandoned
your site without making a purchase and gently remind
them why you’re great and why they should come back?
Do you think you could drive more purchases? Absolutely.
That’s the power of Remarketing.
Remarketing is also known by another
name - site retargeting. You can also
strategically show ads only to people who are
searching for what you sell, but who haven’t found your site
yet, through search retargeting. We’ll get to that later, let’s
start with remarketing or site retargeting.
dcsLOCAL
To Give An Extra Nudge
Show your ads to people searching for
your exact product or service. This one
is obvious. No explanation needed.
To Widen Your Market
Show your ads to people who search
for related products. What products do
people purchase right before buying
your product or service? If you run a
tanning salon it might be a good idea
to run ads to people who’ve recently
searched for “caribbean cruise.” Those
folks are likely to look for tanning
salons next.
To Swipe Business From
Your Competitors
Show your ads to people who search
for your competitors. If someone is
searching for a competitor, you can
follow them around and possibly
convert them. This doesn’t always
work, but it can. It typically works best
in industries where people don’t have
strong brand preferences. For example
if you were tasked with selling the
latest android phone, you might not
be best served to target people who
search for “iphone 6”. But you might
want to target those who search for
“iphone vs. android.”
THREE WAYS TO USE SEARCH
RETARGETING
Site Retargeting
When visitors leave without taking action they leave for
different reasons. Maybe they didn’t have time to make
a purchase. Maybe they are looking at different options
and they left to do some comparison shopping. Maybe
they were just dreaming and shopping for fun and had no
intention of buying (like me visiting the Lamborghini store
when I travel). Maybe they got frustrated with your site.
Maybe an emergency came up. Who knows?
What’s important is that something brought them to your
site, so they must have some interest in what you offer - and
something caused them to leave without making a purchase.
Not all site visitors are worth the same to you, because not
all visitors come to your site with the same intentions. Some
visitors come thinking they might purchase if they like what
they see. Those visitors are worth quite a bit. Some visitors
come just merely to “kick tires” and aren’t really ready or
interested in buying right now. Those visitors obviously
aren’t worth as much to you, but they are still valuable.
Wouldn’t you like to be able to spend more on and follow more
aggressively those who are really interested vs. those who are
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 43 of 48 --

44 | August 2013
marginally interested? Wouldn’t it be great to also tailor your
message based on the actions someone takes on your site?
That’s where retargeting can get pretty advanced. You can tailor
your retargeting messages based on what pages someone
visits, how much time they spend on the site, and whether they
put items in a shopping cart or begin the checkout process.
You can set up your remarketing campaign to aggressively
target visitors who got further in the process. After all
someone who loads up a shopping cart and then leaves
is probably worth more than someone who just visits the
home page and bounces. Basically a remarketing campaign
creates lists of visitors to your site. Visitors who only visit
your home page are in one list, visitors who make it to
your order page, but don’t complete it are in another list.
The options here are endless. Each list then will trigger a
different ad to be displayed.
Facebook Retargeting
Most remarketing is set up so that your ads show up on
news sites, blog sites, and interest sites that participate in
the Google Ad network (or other ad networks). Now you can
also set up your ads to target site visitors on Facebook. These
ads can show up on the right rail of Facebook or even in the
news feed. Results show that retargeting ads that show up
in the newsfeed of Facebook have a 20 to 40x higher click
through rate than a standard remarketing display ad.
Search Retargeting: Banner
Advertising with Minimal Waste
By now hopefully you’re seeing the power of remarketing.
But what about interested people who never get to your site
in the first place? Site retargeting will never reach would-be
prospects who don’t visit your site. Wouldn’t it be great to
only run display ads for people who are looking for what
you sell? That’s where search retargeting comes in. Search
retargeting allows you to show display ads only to people
who have made a search query related to your business.
So if you sell handbags, you could show display ads only
to people who have recently searched for “handbags” on
Google, Bing, or Yahoo. Pretty cool, right?
Getting Started
The easiest way to get started with site retargeting is with a
Google Remarketing campaign. You can set these campaigns
up in your adwords account. Just like adwords you can set
up your remarketing campaign on a cost-per-click basis.
Meaning you only pay when someone clicks on your ad - so
impressions or the branding components are basically free.
Currently costs per click for remarketing are right around $1
in most cases. And that’s even for industries with fairly high
costs per click. We have a client doing well with adwords
who pays $11 per click for search ads and their remarketing
costs hover right around $1 per click. And the traffic that
comes from remarketing is usually very engaged. They
usually stay longer, click more, and covert at a higher rate. A
recent test had retargeting traffic completing goals at 11%...
that’s a huge percentage. There are really no minimums
here, but your site does need at least 100 visitors before your
remarketing ads will start running.
Currently Google only offers site retargeting. They
don’t offer search retargeting. We have a few vendors
we recommend for search retargeting, but it changes
frequently and some vendors have pretty large minimum
spend requirements. If you email me I’m happy to give you
the vendor of choice for your needs.
In my opinion after working and consulting with hundreds
of online businesses, I believe site retargeting is a must for
almost everyone online, and search retargeting can be a big
win for most businesses. DCS
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 44 of 48 --

By Malcolm Robinson, MalcolmRobinsonsBlog.com
In part one of this series I covered the seven elements of
e-mails that sell. This was really the skeleton of it all and
the basic principles on which a good e-mail campaign
can be built. Today I want to start to look into the “nitty
gritty” of what can make an e-mail campaign a raging
success or a miserable failure.
As with anything in life in order to excel or succeed at
you need to practice. Just watching Tiger Woods play
golf won’t ever make you a good golfer. It may give
you a better understanding of the game as well as the
dynamics of the golf swing, but it certainly won’t make
you a pro golfer. In fact the first time you go out and hit
golf balls you will probably suck (sorry but you will).
Just knowing how something works doesn’t make you
good at it. Yes it is the first step in understanding the
process, but that’s exactly what it is, a step. In order
EMAILS THAT
dcs-X
WHY STRONG COPY IS
ESSENTIAL FOR A SUCCESSFUL
EMAIL CAMPAIGN (PART 2)
DotComSecrets Labs | 45
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 45 of 48 --

46 | August 2013
to develop the skills necessary to become proficient at
something you need to practice...
With internet marketing and in particular e-mail
marketing there is one skill you absolutely have to
become proficient at... and that is
copywriting. It’s a
“must” - not an option.
If you become good at copywriting you literally develop
a license to print money. You see the copy that you write
in your emails does one incredibly important thing. It
conveys a message. This isn’t just any message. It’s a
message which results in action. The primary purpose of
your email is to get your lead to take some kind of action.
It may be to go and comment on a blog post you have
written, it may be to complete a questionnaire or join a
Facebook group. It may even be as simple as clicking a link.
Good copywriting tells a story, it takes the reader on a
journey and this is where the true power lies. People love
stories, especially stories they can relate to. If you want to
convey a message the best way to do it is through a story
because stories evoke emotion. A good story will take
the reader on an emotional rollercoaster. They will revisit
the highs and be pulled down by the reminders of the
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 46 of 48 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 47
lows. They can feel both sad and happy in the space of a
few lines. They can feel both desperate and inspired by
reading a few well written words.
This is where the true power of copy lies...
By playing on emotions you are able to make people
aware of their fears, their joys, as well as their needs and
desires. They do this through relating to your story, to
your message.
Now, you hear me going on and on about emotion but
please bear with me, its important. Many of our decisions in
life are made from a place of emotions (including buying).
Now to bring this all back into perspective, how does this
relate to internet marketing? Well it’s simple.
Understanding the importance of good copywriting
and how it enables you to tell a story which conveys a
message which results in action allows you to use it to
your advantage.
The needs and desires within our niche are often the
same and most people will be able to relate to them.
They are to create financial freedom, to escape from the
fear of debt, maybe to become self employed and have
more time freedom, to be able to travel with your family
instead of wondering where the next mortgage payment
is coming from.
Evoke these needs and desires in a non threatening way
by allowing people to relate to your story and you are
90% of the way there. You see at this point when their
emotions are high, and they are fully aware of their needs
and desires, you are able to present a solution. They will
see it as value and will be so incredibly thankful that you
shared it with them.
And it goes without saying that this solution = $$ for you.
Next month I am going to be delving into the process of
developing your “attractive character.” This truly is one of
the fundamentals of successful e-mail marketing and is
something you mustn’t miss.
In the meantime start reading successful marketers’
emails and take note of how and what they write. There
is logic behind every word, and soon you will start to
see a pattern.
Then practice... DCS
Good copywriting tells a story, it takes the reader on a
journey and this is where the true power lies. People love
stories, especially stories they can relate to.”
dcsX	www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

Presentation or Webinar (pg 3)
+$10 MILLION in sales over 8 years!

+Ours each did $1 MILLION/year!
Get Your Leads to Call Your Sales Team
First (pg 9)
+$300 MORE dollars per lead!
Insert a Lightbox Popup (pg 16)
+67% MORE optins!
Post a Special Coupon Offer for
Facebook Fans (pg 17)
+19% MORE sales!
Showcase a Big Smiling Customer and
Their Testimonial (pg 22)
+103% INCREASE in conversions!
Use “Dirty Little Secret” in an Email
Subject Line (pg 30)
+122% MORE unique click-throughs!
Add A Webinar Recap (pg 28)
+120% MORE sales!
Replace Percent Off Discounts With
“Dollars Off” In Emails (pg 33)
+22% INCREASE in conversions!
Get Subscribers to Sign Up Through a
Major Social Network (pg 33)
+Leads worth 80X MORE!

Ads (pg 34)

Targeted Ads on Other Sites They Visit
(pg 42)
